title: GADT Expression Evaluator
code:
type _ expr =
  | Int : int -> int expr
  | Bool : bool -> bool expr
  | Add : int expr * int expr -> int expr
  | If : bool expr * 'a expr * 'a expr -> 'a expr
  | Eq : int expr * int expr -> bool expr

let rec eval : type a. a expr -> a = function
  | Int n -> n
  | Bool b -> b
  | Add (x, y) -> eval x + eval y
  | If (cond, t, f) -> if eval cond then eval t else eval f
  | Eq (x, y) -> eval x = eval y

let () = Printf.printf "%d\n" (eval (If (Eq (Int 1, Int 1), Add (Int 2, Int 3), Int 0)))
description: A type-safe expression evaluator using Generalized Algebraic Data Types (GADTs). The type index on `expr` guarantees that `eval` returns `int` for `Add` and `bool` for `Eq` — ill-typed expressions are rejected at compile time.
